items.title: Cloud credentials directory created without mode 0700 — API tokens world-listable on multi-user systems
items.intent: The cloud credentials directory must not be listable by other users — it stores API tokens and must be created with mode 0700 so directory contents are not exposed, matching the protection applied to credentials.ts file itself.
items.description: src/cloud/provisioning/credentials.ts line 53 calls mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true }) without a mode option when creating the cloud/ directory. This creates the directory with default permissions (0755), making it world-readable. While credentials.json is written with mode 0600 (correct), any user on the system can ls the cloud/ directory to enumerate that cloud credentials exist. Same pattern: registry.ts line 57 and snapshot-registry.ts line 51 both call mkdirSync without mode. registry.ts is already covered by BUG-077. snapshot-registry.ts stores non-sensitive data (provider, region, version, timestamp) but should be consistent. Fix: pass { recursive: true, mode: 0o700 } to all three mkdirSync calls.
steps: Read src/cloud/provisioning/credentials.ts lines 50-55 — confirm mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true }) has no mode option
Change mkdirSync in credentials.ts to mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true, mode: 0o700 })
Read src/cloud/provisioning/snapshot-registry.ts lines 48-53 — confirm mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true }) has no mode option
Change mkdirSync in snapshot-registry.ts to mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true, mode: 0o700 })
Run credentials tests and snapshot-registry tests — confirm both pass with no regressions
acceptanceCriteria: The cloud/ directory (deployDir/cloud/) is created with mode 0700 — stat confirms no group/other read bits
snapshot-registry parent directory is created with mode 0700
File permissions on credentials.json and snapshots.json remain unchanged at 0600
items.groomingNotes: RE-GROOMED 2026-03-23 (attempt 2 — failure analysis). FAIL PATTERN: previous steps said 'Run credentials tests and snapshot-registry tests' but no such test files exist (only registry.test.ts, engine.test.ts, ssh-keygen.test.ts, cloud-init.test.ts, provider tests). Also: steps did not mention the import changes required — DIR_MODE_SECRET must be added to the import statement in both files before it can be used. THREE PRECISE CHANGES: (1) credentials.ts line 13: add DIR_MODE_SECRET to the existing import — change 'import { FILE_MODE_SECRET }' to 'import { DIR_MODE_SECRET, FILE_MODE_SECRET }'. (2) credentials.ts line 53: change mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true }) to mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true, mode: DIR_MODE_SECRET }). (3) snapshot-registry.ts has NO import from defaults.ts — add 'import { DIR_MODE_SECRET } from "../../config/defaults.js";' near top with other imports, then change mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true }) at line 51 to mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true, mode: DIR_MODE_SECRET }). TESTING: No credentials.test.ts or snapshot-registry.test.ts exist — create both following registry.test.ts pattern: mkdtempSync tmpdir, call setProviderCredential() / addSnapshot(), statSync the cloud/ dir, assert mode & 0o777 === 0o700. Then run npm test to confirm no regressions. DO NOT add mode to snapshot file writes — snapshots.json is non-sensitive, only the directory needs 0700.

items.title: Integration credentials directory created without mode 0700 — engine/ dir world-listable
items.intent: The engine/ directory must not be world-listable. It stores integration credentials and must be created with mode 0700 so directory contents cannot be enumerated, matching the protection applied to credentials.json itself.
items.description: src/secure/credentials/credential-store.ts line 60 calls mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true }) without a mode option, creating deployDir/engine/ with default permissions (0755). The engine/ directory contains credentials.json (integration API keys — IMAP passwords, Telegram tokens, OAuth secrets) written with mode 0600 (correct), but any user on the system can ls the engine/ directory to enumerate that integration credentials exist. Fix: add DIR_MODE_SECRET to the import from defaults.js and pass { recursive: true, mode: DIR_MODE_SECRET } to the mkdirSync call.
items.groomingNotes: READY. Same pattern as BUG-077 and BUG-078. PRECISE STEPS: (1) Read credential-store.ts line 15 — note 'import { FILE_MODE_SECRET }' from defaults.js, DIR_MODE_SECRET is NOT imported. (2) In credential-store.ts line 15, add DIR_MODE_SECRET: change 'import { FILE_MODE_SECRET }' to 'import { DIR_MODE_SECRET, FILE_MODE_SECRET }'. (3) In credential-store.ts line 60, change mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true }) to mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true, mode: DIR_MODE_SECRET }). (4) No credential-store.test.ts exists — create src/secure/credentials/credential-store.test.ts following registry.test.ts pattern: mkdtempSync, call writeCredentialStore() with a minimal credential store, statSync the engine/ parent dir, assert mode & 0o777 === 0o700. (5) Run npm test to confirm all tests pass. AC: stat on deployDir/engine/ shows drwx------ (0700). File mode on credentials.json remains 0600 unchanged.

items.title: .env file parent directory created without mode 0700 — .env secrets world-listable
items.intent: The directory containing .env files must not be world-listable. Create it with mode 0700 so secrets cannot be enumerated by other users, matching the protection applied to the .env file itself.
items.description: src/secure/credentials/env-store.ts line 117 calls mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true }) without a mode option, creating the .env file's parent directory with default permissions (0755). .env files are written with mode 0600 (correct) but any user on the system can ls the directory to enumerate that a .env file exists. Same class of vulnerability as BUG-077, BUG-078, and BUG-081. Fix: add DIR_MODE_SECRET to the import from defaults.js (line 15 already imports FILE_MODE_SECRET) and pass { recursive: true, mode: DIR_MODE_SECRET } to the mkdirSync call at line 117.
items.groomingNotes: READY. Same pattern as BUG-077, BUG-078, BUG-081. PRECISE STEPS: (1) Read env-store.ts line 15 — confirm 'import { FILE_MODE_SECRET }' from defaults.js, DIR_MODE_SECRET is NOT imported. (2) In env-store.ts line 15, add DIR_MODE_SECRET: change 'import { FILE_MODE_SECRET }' to 'import { DIR_MODE_SECRET, FILE_MODE_SECRET }'. (3) In env-store.ts line 117, change mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true }) to mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true, mode: DIR_MODE_SECRET }). (4) env-store.test.ts already exists and tests file mode 0600 — add ONE new test: 'creates parent directory with mode 0700'. Pattern: mkdtempSync, define a subdirectory path (dir/engine/.env), call writeEnvAtomic with an empty EnvFile, statSync the parent dir (dir/engine/), assert mode & 0o777 === 0o700. (5) Run npm test to confirm all tests pass. AC: stat on the .env parent directory shows drwx------ (0700). File mode on .env remains 0600 unchanged.

items.title: Fleet registry directory (cloud/) created without mode 0700 — agent paths world-listable
items.intent: The cloud/ directory must always be created with mode 0700 regardless of which code path initializes it first. fleet.json initialization must not downgrade the directory to world-listable.
items.description: src/cloud/fleet/discovery.ts line 58 calls mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true }) without a mode option, creating deployDir/cloud/ with default permissions (0755) when fleet is initialized before cloud credentials. fleet.json stores agent deployment directory paths — on a multi-user system, any user can ls the cloud/ directory to learn that fleet registration exists. BUG-078 fixes cloud/ for the credentials path but only when writeCloudCredentials() runs first; if fleet is registered before any credentials are stored, cloud/ is created at 0755 by discovery.ts. Fix: add DIR_MODE_SECRET import from defaults.js and pass { recursive: true, mode: DIR_MODE_SECRET } to mkdirSync at line 58. Note: no import from defaults.ts exists in discovery.ts currently.
items.groomingNotes: READY. Same pattern as BUG-077, BUG-078, BUG-081, BUG-082. PRECISE STEPS: (1) Read discovery.ts lines 1-15 — confirm there is NO import from defaults.ts. (2) Add import: 'import { DIR_MODE_SECRET } from "../../config/defaults.js";' to the import block in discovery.ts (after the paths.js import, maintaining alphabetical order — place between config/paths.js and cloud/heartbeat/reporter.js imports is fine). (3) In discovery.ts line 58, change mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true }) to mkdirSync(dir, { recursive: true, mode: DIR_MODE_SECRET }). (4) No fleet/discovery.test.ts exists — do NOT create one for this bug. Run npm test to confirm no regressions. SCOPE: single file, single line change plus one import. AC: stat on deployDir/cloud/ shows drwx------ (0700) when fleet.js creates the directory before credentials.ts runs.
